splits.title: A Prince of Ur
splits.author: Homespun
splits.start_boundary: A Prince of Ur. By Homespun. The long, winding stone
splits.end_boundary: only in the pure white linen of his tunic, while upon the flowing dark brown of his glossy hair there rested only the small skull-cap which was but a covering for the crown of his blossoming head. His noble features were regular, his nose had that full and long contour that bent as the line swept down his face to give the full benignity which his glowing brown eyes with the covert kindliness in their depths warranted. The mouth was large and firm, as befitted such a prince, and on the firm, clear-cut chin, there sat the seal of self-command. He justified the incoherent epithets of praise which were preceding the voluble visitor up the long and tiresome stairs.

files.notes: The file contains 4 articles that were merged together. The 'A Prince of Ur' article appears to be cut off mid-sentence at the end of the file, suggesting this is part of a larger serialized story.

files.notes: This file contains the continuation of 'A Prince of Ur' serialized story and the 'Current Topics' section. There's also a Relief Society conference announcement between the two main articles. The file appears to end with the beginning of the Editorial section listing the General Board members.

files.notes: This file contains four distinct lesson sections: Work and Business (Lesson I), Genealogy (Lesson III), Home Ethics, and Art. The Theology lesson (Lesson II) mentioned in the header appears to be the 'Keeping the Sabbath Day Holy' content which is part of the Work and Business section. There's also a musical composition 'The Relief Society' by John M. Chamberlain that appears to be part of the Art lesson section.
